title: Eat the — drawing-fragment Apple Note
html:
TL;DR

  • June 1, 2020 Apple Note titled "Eat the" — body is a single character (b;) followed by an embedded drawing-attachment reference (com.apple.drawing.2). The text is a fragment; the content is whatever Yahir sketched.
  • Captures the 's handling of non text capture: Apple Notes preserves the drawing as a binary attachment; the osascript exporter surfaces only the placeholder, leaving a textual ghost of a visual artifact.
  • Date sits squarely in the COVID summer 2020 window, two weeks after George Floyd protests began — the title "Eat the" (likely eat the rich) reads as a meme-era political-mood marker.
  • Pairs as a fragment-class note with the "olivecity877" / "WS" note four months later — both are typo-grade Apple-Notes residue from the COVID era junk drawer.
  • Documents a structural export tool limitation worth a follow-up densify check: drawings in Apple Notes do not round-trip into the vault as renderable images — they survive only as opaque IDs.

Note

"Eat the" / b; / [drawing]. Probably Eat the rich — the meme-phrase at peak resonance in spring-summer 2020. Probably never finished. Probably a doodle next to the words.

The note's value is structural, not content: it shows the exporter's blind spot. Any Apple-Notes drawing in the corpus arrives as a com.apple.drawing.N placeholder — searchable text exists, the visual does not. For densify passes that try to mine the corpus exhaustively, this is a known gap; either accept the loss or build a separate drawing-export pipeline.

The note itself is a COVID summer timestamp — a half-typed phrase saved alongside a drawing during the months Yahir was 17 and home in Grants Pass. Whatever the doodle was, it's gone now.

Connections

  • — form
  • osascript as export tool — known drawing-loss limitation
  • recording as note — non-text capture form
  • covid pandemic era — temporal cluster
  • 2020 10 23 olivecity877 — sibling fragment-class note
